message:
Time to put an old myth to rest: dogs do NOT see the world in black and white. 🐾

Here's what's really going on. Our eyes have three types of color sensors; dogs have two. That means they see the world in shades of blue and yellow, while reds and greens tend to wash out into murky browns and grays. It's actually a lot like how a person with red-green color blindness sees. So that bright red ball you toss onto the green lawn? To your dog, it can practically vanish into the grass — they're often tracking it by movement and smell, not color!

But before you feel sorry for them, know this: dogs win in other ways. They're far better at catching the tiniest motion, and they see beautifully in dim light, when our eyes are basically useless. 💛

Fun little experiment: try a blue or yellow toy next time and watch how much easier your dog spots it.

What color is your dog's favorite toy? Now you might know why they love it! 🎾🐶
